types.name: AlignmentGeometry
types.documentation:
Base class for [Alignment] that allows for text-direction aware resolution.

A property or argument of this type accepts classes created either with [new
Alignment] and its variants or [new AlignmentDirectional].

To convert an [AlignmentGeometry] object of indeterminate type into an
[Alignment] object, call the [resolve] method.

types.name: EdgeInsetsGeometry
types.documentation:
Base class for [EdgeInsets] that allows for text-direction aware resolution.

A property or argument of this type accepts classes created either with [new
EdgeInsets] and its variants or [new EdgeInsetsDirectional].

To convert an [EdgeInsetsGeometry] object of indeterminate type into an
[EdgeInsets] object, call the [resolve] method.

types.name: BorderRadiusGeometry
types.documentation:
Base class for [BorderRadius] that allows for text-direction aware resolution.

A property or argument of this type accepts classes created either with [new
BorderRadius] and its variants or [new BorderRadiusDirectional].

To convert a [BorderRadiusGeometry] object of indeterminate type into a
[BorderRadius] object, call the [resolve] method.

types.name: Gradient
types.documentation:
A 2D gradient.

This is an interface that allows subclasses to be created for each of the
different ways that the shaders can be created.

See also:

 * [LinearGradient], which displays a gradient in parallel lines, and has an
   example which shows a different way to use [Gradient] objects.
 * [RadialGradient], which displays a gradient in concentric circles, and
   has an example which shows a different way to use [Gradient] objects.
 * [SweepGradient], which displays a gradient in a sweeping arc around a
   center point.
 * [BoxDecoration], which can take a [Gradient] in its
   [BoxDecoration.gradient] property.

types.name: Animation
types.documentation:
An animation with a value of type T.

An animation consists of a value (of type T) together with a status. The
status indicates whether the animation is conceptually running from beginning
to end or from the end back to the beginning, although the actual value of the
animation might not change monotonically (e.g., if the animation uses a curve
that bounces).

Animations also let other objects listen for changes to either their value or
their status. These callbacks are called during the "animation" phase of the
pipeline, just prior to rebuilding widgets.

types.name: Decoration
types.documentation:
A description of a box decoration (a decoration applied to a [Rect]).

This class presents the abstract interface for all decorations.
See [BoxDecoration] for a concrete example.

To actually paint a [Decoration], use the [createBoxPainter] method to obtain
a [BoxPainter]. [Decoration] objects can be shared between boxes; [BoxPainter]
objects can cache resources to make painting on a particular surface faster.

types.name: Constraints
types.documentation:
Immutable layout constraints for [RenderBox] layout.

A [Size] respects a [BoxConstraints] if, and only if, all of the following
relations hold:

* [minWidth] <= [Size.width] <= [maxWidth]
* [minHeight] <= [Size.height] <= [maxHeight]

The constraints themselves must satisfy these relations:

* 0.0 <= [minWidth] <= [maxWidth] <= [double.infinity]
* 0.0 <= [minHeight] <= [maxHeight] <= [double.infinity]

types.name: BoxBorder
types.documentation:
Base class for box borders that can paint a rectangle.

The four sides of a [BoxBorder] are represented by [BorderSide] objects, and
each side can have a different color and width.

[BoxBorder] is an interface that allows subclasses to implement different
ways to paint borders. The most common implementation is [Border], which
paints the four sides with uniform or different colors and widths.

types.name: ParametricCurve
types.documentation:
An abstract class providing an interface for parametric curves.

A parametric curve is a function from a unitless time parameter (typically in the range 0.0 to 1.0) to a dimensional value (typically in the range 0.0 to 1.0). Parametric curves are defined to have the same value at 0.0 and 1.0.

types.name: FocusNode
types.documentation: An object that can be used by a stateful widget to obtain the keyboard focus and to handle keyboard events. This is a Flutter SDK class that cannot be marshaled through FFI. Widgets using FocusNode should be implemented with manual wrappers.

types.name: GlobalKey
types.documentation: A key that is unique across the entire app. This is a Flutter SDK class that cannot be marshaled through FFI. Widgets using GlobalKey should be implemented with manual wrappers.

types.name: ScrollPhysics
types.documentation: Determines the physics of a Scrollable widget. This is a Flutter SDK class that cannot be marshaled through FFI. Widgets using ScrollPhysics should be implemented with manual wrappers.

types.name: BoxConstraints
types.documentation: Immutable layout constraints for RenderBox layout.

properties.name: minWidth
properties.documentation: The minimum width that satisfies the constraints.

properties.name: maxWidth
properties.documentation: The maximum width that satisfies the constraints.

properties.name: minHeight
properties.documentation: The minimum height that satisfies the constraints.

properties.name: maxHeight
properties.documentation: The maximum height that satisfies the constraints.

types.name: TextStyle
types.documentation: An immutable style describing how to format and paint text.

properties.name: inherit
properties.documentation: Whether to inherit from parent TextStyle (1 = true).

properties.name: color
properties.documentation: The color to use when painting the text (0 means null).

properties.name: backgroundColor
properties.documentation: The color to use as the background for the text (0 means null).

properties.name: fontFamily
properties.documentation: The name of the font to use when painting the text.

properties.name: fontSize
properties.documentation: The size of glyphs (in logical pixels) to use when painting the text.

properties.name: fontWeight
properties.documentation: The typeface thickness to use when painting the text.

properties.name: fontStyle
properties.documentation: The typeface variant to use when drawing the letters.

properties.name: letterSpacing
properties.documentation: The amount of space to add between each letter.

properties.name: wordSpacing
properties.documentation: The amount of space to add at each sequence of white-space.

properties.name: textBaseline
properties.documentation: The common baseline that should be aligned between this text span and its parent text span.

properties.name: height
properties.documentation: The height of this text span, as a multiple of the font size.

properties.name: decorationThickness
properties.documentation: The thickness of the decoration stroke as a multiplier of the thickness defined by the font.

properties.name: debugLabel
properties.documentation: A human-readable description of this text style.

types.name: ImageProvider
types.documentation: Identifies an image without committing to the precise final asset.
